name: Ms. Bumbleflower
tagline: Jolrael alpha strike (lands -> X/X, X = hand size)
idea: Cast a high volume of cheap spells.

briefKills.line: Jolrael, Mwonvuli Recluse + a full hand + a board (Cats / payoff creatures / stolen bodies) → Jolrael's 4GG pump sets your team to X/X (X = cards in hand) → 8–12-power alpha
Willbreaker + aim Bumbleflower's counter at an opp's creature each cast → steal a creature every spell (it flies in that turn) → swing with their own board

openings: Weakest axis — Kill reliability (3/5): the judges' soft spot.
Tables (all three) never-close 2% of the time.

clock.caption: Decap (one opponent) lands around T8; the full table around T11.

gamePlan: Cast a high volume of cheap spells. Each cast triggers Bumbleflower, and the deck is built so that every part of that trigger feeds a payoff: the forced opponent-draw feeds "opponents drawing" payoffs, the second-resolution draw-two feeds "you drawing" payoffs, and the +1/+1 counter feeds "counters placed" payoffs — or steals a creature outright. The result is a tempo-control deck that buries the table in card advantage while picking apart their board, then closes slowly with an evasive, counter-pumped threat, a Jolrael alpha strike, or a stolen army.
winLine: Jolrael alpha strike (lands -> X/X, X = hand size)

finishers.name: Jolrael Alpha Strike
finishers.note: With a full hand (routine for this deck) and a board of Cats, payoff creatures, and/or stolen bodies, Jolrael's {4}{G}{G} sets your team to X/X where X = cards in hand.

finishers.name: Willbreaker Theft, then Swing
finishers.note: With Willbreaker in play, aim Bumbleflower's counter at an opponent's creature each cast to steal it (and it gains flying that turn — can attack immediately).

finishers.name: Evasive Counter Beats
finishers.note: A single creature (commander or a payoff body) accumulates a +1/+1 counter and flying every cast.

rulings.name: Bumbleflower's ability resolves BEFORE the spell that triggered it, and resolves even if that spell is countered.
rulings.note: The draw / counter / steal happens first — so a Willbreaker steal off your spell goes through even if the spell itself gets countered.

rulings.name: The "draw two" is on the second resolution each turn
rulings.note: cast at least two spells you trigger on. Free spells (Probe, Snap, Frantic Search) are how you reliably get there.

rulings.name: The counter and flying go to the same target creature; you pick it every cast.
rulings.note: Aim it at: (a) your evasive threat, (b) Dusk Legion Duelist / Fathom Mage to draw a card, or (c) an opponent's creature with Willbreaker out to steal it. You keep stolen creatures only while Willbreaker lives.

rulings.name: Willbreaker triggers on any of your targeted effects hitting an opponent's creature
rulings.note: Bumbleflower's counter, Ty Lee's tap, Generous Patron's support. Pongify/Path/Swords also target, but they destroy; use the non-lethal effects to steal.

rulings.name: The forced opponent-draw is a real cost — but you choose who.
rulings.note: Feed the least threatening player, or feed someone to turn on Faerie Mastermind (you draw on their 2nd draw), Smuggler's Share (draw per opponent who drew 2+), and Tataru Taru (Treasure when an opponent draws on your turn). Don't gift a draw-punisher (Nekusar) for free.

rulings.name: Jolrael's pump sets base P/T to cards-in-hand and overwrites
rulings.note: play it as a finisher with a full hand. Her Cat tokens come from your second draw each turn (which the commander's draw-two enables).

rulings.name: Dress Down turns off ALL creature abilities, including yours
rulings.note: use it to blank an opponent's engine/combo, not while you're relying on your own creatures; it sacrifices itself at end step.

rulings.name: Generous Gift / Beast Within hand the target's controller a token
rulings.note: fine as removal, just count the body you're giving back.

1 Ms. Bumbleflower

mull.strategy: Looking for: lands + ramp + a couple of cheap spells, with Bumbleflower reachable by T3–4. An all-interaction hand with no payoff and no route to the commander is a toss, however pretty the curve — the engine only pays once Bumbleflower plus a payoff are converting the cheap spells into cards and bodies.

killTree.title: Ms. Bumbleflower — Ms. Bumbleflower
killTree.root:
Ms. Bumbleflower online (~T3–4)
each spell you cast: an opp draws, +1/+1 counter (+flying) on a creature;
the 2nd cast each turn → you draw two
killTree.stall:
out-value the table — chain cantrips for the 2nd-cast draw-two, deploy draw payoffs,
hold up the deep counter/removal suite; the kill is slow & combat-only, so close from dominance
killTree.caption: cheapest line first · fall to the next

lines.need:
Jolrael, Mwonvuli Recluse + a full hand + a board
(Cats / payoff creatures / stolen bodies)
lines.kill: Jolrael's 4GG pump sets your team to X/X (X = cards in hand) → 8–12-power alpha

lines.need: Willbreaker + aim Bumbleflower's counter at an opp's creature each cast
lines.kill: steal a creature every spell (it flies in that turn) → swing with their own board

lines.need:
pile +1/+1 counters & flying on one carrier each cast
(optionally vacuum them into Sin, Unending Cataclysm) (fallback)
lines.kill: a single evasive counter-grown threat flies over
